name: DES-1A "Dustwalker"
description: The entry-level deep sifting unit, the Dustwalker is an industrial-scale regolith processor derived from terrestrial mineral separation technology. Twin counter-rotating centrifugal drums accept raw regolith from an intake hopper, spinning at differential speeds to separate fine ice grains and volatile-bearing particles from bulk silicate waste. A modest 400 K bake-out chamber downstream sublimates trapped water molecules from the concentrate, which are captured by a passive radiative cryocondenser. At 10 tonnes dry mass, the Dustwalker is the heaviest T1 equipment in the ISRU family — a consequence of the massive drum assemblies and reinforced intake conveyors needed to process 5 tonnes of raw regolith per hour. Power draw is remarkably low at 0.3 MW, as the drums are spun by mechanical gearing off a single electric motor and the bake-out temperature requires only low-grade waste heat. Designed for the lunar highlands and other dry sites where water ice concentration is minimal but non-zero.
